steps.title: Define your editorial concept
steps.description: Share your artistic vision—describe the outdoor setting like open fields, gardens, or natural landscapes, and the mood you want to create.

steps.title: Choose fashion style and aesthetic
steps.description: Select the type of fashion—from flowing dresses and haute couture to romantic and ethereal styles that complement natural outdoor backdrops.

steps.title: Generate AI editorial imagery
steps.description: Use AI to produce artistic fashion photography with natural lighting, elegant poses, and dreamy outdoor atmospheres reminiscent of high-fashion editorials.

items.question: What is the AI Fashion Outdoor Shorts workflow?
items.answer: AI Fashion Outdoor Shorts is an AI workflow for creating artistic editorial fashion imagery in outdoor settings. It generates elegant, magazine-quality photography featuring fashion in natural landscapes with dreamy, artistic aesthetics.

items.question: Who is this template designed for?
items.answer: This template is ideal for fashion brands, creative agencies, photographers, stylists, and content creators who want to produce editorial-style fashion imagery in outdoor settings.

items.question: What input do I need to start?
items.answer: You describe your editorial concept, including the outdoor setting (fields, gardens, natural landscapes), the fashion style (flowing dresses, haute couture, romantic styles), and the artistic mood you want to achieve.

items.question: What type of output does this workflow produce?
items.answer: The workflow produces AI-generated editorial fashion photography with natural lighting, elegant poses, and artistic outdoor atmospheres—similar to high-fashion magazine editorials.

items.question: Can I customize the outdoor setting and style?
items.answer: Yes, you can specify various natural settings like open fields, flower gardens, countryside landscapes, and choose different fashion aesthetics from ethereal and romantic to bold and avant-garde.

items.question: Where can I use the generated content?
items.answer: You can use the outputs for brand lookbooks, fashion campaigns, magazine submissions, social media content, mood boards, and creative presentations.

items.question: How do credits and pricing work?
items.answer: Running AI workflows in OpenCreator consumes credits based on the underlying image models and generation parameters. You can start with test generations and scale once satisfied with the results.
